title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
Your school's digital literacy committee has asked you to write a report on students' use of social media. Include information about which platforms students use most, how social media affects their wellbeing, and recommendations for promoting healthier social media habits.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Student Social Media Use at Millbrook School

Introduction
This report summarises findings from a survey of 175 students regarding their social media habits and the impact on their wellbeing, with recommendations for the school's digital literacy programme.

Most Used Platforms
Instagram and TikTok were by far the most popular platforms, used daily by 81% and 74% of respondents respectively. YouTube and Snapchat were also widely used. The average student reported spending 3.5 hours per day on social media.

Impact on Wellbeing
The results revealed a complex picture. While many students value social media for staying in touch with friends and discovering new interests, 58% reported feeling worse about themselves after extended use, citing comparisons with influencers and peer pressure to present a perfect image. Several students linked heavy social media use to sleep problems and difficulty concentrating on schoolwork.

Recommendations
The committee should introduce digital wellbeing workshops covering topics such as screen time management, recognising manipulative content, and the curated nature of social media. Setting up a peer support programme where older students share strategies for healthy online habits is also strongly recommended.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Morgan is starting a small business and wants to use social media to promote it. Write an email to Morgan giving advice on which platforms to use, how to create engaging content, and what mistakes to avoid when using social media for business.

Write your email to Morgan.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Morgan,

How exciting that the business is getting started! Social media can be an incredibly powerful tool for promoting it, as long as you approach it strategically. Here are a few thoughts.

First, don't try to be on every platform at once. Choose one or two that are most relevant to your audience. If you're targeting younger customers, Instagram and TikTok are ideal. For a more professional audience, LinkedIn is worth considering. Focus your energy where your potential customers actually spend their time.

In terms of content, consistency is everything. Post regularly — even three times a week is enough to build an audience — and make sure your content is genuinely useful or entertaining rather than purely promotional. Behind-the-scenes content and personal stories tend to perform particularly well.

As for mistakes to avoid: never buy followers (it destroys credibility), always respond to comments and messages promptly, and be very careful about what you post because once something is online, it is very difficult to remove.

Good luck with everything — I can't wait to see it grow!

All the best,
Tess

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'Social media: a force for good or a threat to society? Write an article discussing the positive and negative effects of social media and sharing your own view on its overall impact.'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Scrolling Through the Debate: Is Social Media Good or Bad?

Few inventions in history have transformed daily life as rapidly as social media. Within a single generation, platforms like Instagram, Twitter, and TikTok have reshaped how we communicate, consume news, and understand ourselves. But the debate about whether this has been a positive development rages on.

The case for social media is compelling. It has given a voice to people who previously had none, connected isolated individuals with communities that support them, and enabled social movements to organise and demand change with unprecedented speed. It has also created genuine economic opportunities for small businesses and independent creators worldwide.

And yet the downsides are equally real. The spread of misinformation, the addictive design of these platforms, and the well-documented impact on mental health — particularly among young women — cannot be dismissed.

My own view is that social media is neither inherently good nor bad: it is a powerful mirror of society. The challenge is not the technology itself, but ensuring we use it with awareness, purpose, and genuine human connection at its centre.

The scroll must be in our hands, not the algorithm's.
